input.initialPrompt:
I have an ObservableCollection<Product> with Name and Price properties.
Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using
CollectionView with a DataTemplate that shows both the name and price.
Use compiled bindings.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (e.g., `ProductsPage.xaml`)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="YourApp.ProductsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:ProductsViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Products}"
                    ItemsLayout="VerticalList">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:Product">
                <HorizontalStackLayout Padding="10" Spacing="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                           FontSize="16"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}"
                           FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </HorizontalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel & Model**
```csharp
public class Product
{
    public string Name { get; set; }
    public decimal Price { get; set; }
}

public class ProductsViewModel
{
    public ObservableCollection<Product> Products { get; } = new()
    {
        new Product { Name = "Widget",  Price = 9.99m },
        new Product { Name = "Gadget",  Price = 24.95m },
        new Product { Name = "Gizmo",   Price = 14.50m },
    };
}
```

**Key points:**

- **`x:DataType`** on the `ContentPage` enables compiled bindings for the page-level `BindingContext`, and on the `DataTemplate` for each item — this gives compile-time checking and better performance.
- **`CollectionView`** scrolls vertically by default (`VerticalList`).
- **`ObservableCollection`** ensures the UI updates automatically when items are added/removed.
- Set the `BindingContext` in code-behind or via DI: `BindingContext = new ProductsViewModel();`
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) example using CollectionView with compiled bindings. Let me provide a concise, working example.
